selected_need: Keep self-hosting release state synchronized so active runtime artifacts always point to the current release baseline.
context_summary: AOF v3.3 runtime outputs show stale release refs across organization, bootstrap, contract, and roadmap surfaces after a real release.
hypothesis: A canonical active release manifest plus drift detection and a runtime-native refresh path will prevent stale release pointers from surviving after a release.
rejected_alternatives: treat the drift as documentation-only cleanup
fix stale refs manually after each release without runtime support
explicit_risks: repair logic could overwrite intentionally historical refs if active vs archival surfaces are not clearly separated
delivery_validation_requirements: verify that organization-status, contract-register, roadmap-status, and bootstrap surfaces all resolve to the same active release baseline after update
need: Keep self-hosting release state synchronized after each release.
intent: Add runtime-native release-state synchronization and drift detection without weakening existing governance gates.
context: The post-v3.3 self-hosting repo demonstrates active release-state drift that is operationally visible through runtime commands.
